attacking_style: Long-range solo attacks from breakaways on HC climbs, typically launching 10km from summit. Prefers sustained efforts over explosive steep gradients, willing to commit early rather than wait for final kilometers.
team_tactics: Receives tactical positioning and launch support from team on steep gradients, but operates primarily as lone attacker once move is initiated. Team sets him up but doesn't control tempo extensively.
terrain_preference: Long, sustained HC climbs where endurance trumps explosive power. Vulnerable on ultra-steep gradients (20%+) and short, punchy finishes under 2km where pure climbers can respond.
vulnerability: Insufficient explosive power on gradients exceeding 20% and in final 1-2km of climbs. Unable to respond to late accelerations from fresher or more explosive climbers despite earlier efforts.
key_patterns: Launches solo attacks 10km from summit on HC climbs, both observations from breakaway position
Struggles to finish on ultra-steep gradients 20%+, dropped or outsprinted twice in final kilometers
Operates from early breakaways rather than GC group, winning tactic in successful stage
Can hold off GC favorites when gap established early, but vulnerable to pure climbers in direct confrontation
form_trajectory: negative: 2018 observation shows dropped on steep finish despite initiating breakaway attack, suggesting declining ability to compete on selective gradients
